subject: Sociology
title: Digital Surveillance Globally
description: A 60-second lesson on Digital Surveillance Globally.

hook.text: China's social credit system scores citizens on behavior. Dystopian? Many democracies track citizens too.

buildup.text: Governments worldwide use digital surveillance for security, from facial recognition to mass data collection.

discovery.text: Surveillance technologies developed in democracies are exported to authoritarian regimes that use them for repression.

twist.text: The line between safety and control blurs. Anti-terror tools monitor activists, journalists, and ordinary citizens.

climax.text: Global surveillance raises a fundamental question: can freedom survive when every action is watched and recorded?

punchline.text: Security and freedom fight for the same space.

quiz.question: What concern do surveillance technologies raise?
options: They blur the line between security and repressive control
